stem: Solve \( |x - ([[b]])| = [[c]] \). Which of the following is a solution?
distractor_rationales: This is the value that makes the expression inside the bars zero, not a point at distance [[c]] from it.
This overshoots the correct solution by an extra \( [[c]] \).
This overshoots the correct solution by an extra \( [[c]] \) in the other direction.
This is off by one from a genuine solution.
This is off by one from a genuine solution.
This lies far outside both solutions.

stem: For how many integers \( x \) is \( |x - ([[b]])| < [[c]] \) true?
solution: \( |x-([[b]])| < [[c]] \) means \( ([[b]]) - [[c]] < x < ([[b]]) + [[c]] \), an open interval of length \( 2\cdot[[c]] \). The number of integers strictly inside it is \( 2\cdot[[c]] - 1 = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is the length of the interval; an open interval of integer length \( 2c \) contains \( 2c - 1 \) integers, not \( 2c \).
This over-counts by treating the interval as if its endpoints were included.
This under-counts by one from the correct number of integers.
This is only half the interval's worth of integers.
This is larger than the correct count.
This is smaller than the correct count.

stem: What is the smallest integer \( x \) greater than [[b]] for which \( |x - ([[b]])| > [[c]] \)?
solution: For \( x > [[b]] \), \( |x-([[b]])| = x - ([[b]]) \), so the inequality becomes \( x - ([[b]]) > [[c]] \), i.e. \( x > ([[b]]) + [[c]] \). The smallest integer beyond this threshold is \( ([[b]]) + [[c]] + 1 = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This sits exactly at the boundary, where the inequality is not yet strict.
This overshoots the smallest qualifying integer by one.
This looks on the wrong side of [[b]], using the lower branch of the absolute value instead.
This omits [[b]] entirely from the threshold.
This is larger than the correct value.
This is smaller than the correct value.

stem: What is the value of \( x \) that satisfies \( |x - ([[a]])| = |x - ([[b]])| \)?
solution: The point equidistant from \( [[a]] \) and \( [[b]] \) is their midpoint, \( x = \dfrac{[[a]] + [[b]]}{2} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is one of the two given points, not the point equidistant from both.
This is the other given point, not the point equidistant from both.
This is the sum of the two points rather than their average.
This is off by one from the true midpoint.
This is off by one from the true midpoint.
This is off by two from the true midpoint.

stem: What is the minimum possible value of \( |x - ([[a]])| + |x - ([[b]])| \) over all real numbers \( x \)?
solution: The sum of distances from \( x \) to \( [[a]] \) and to \( [[b]] \) is minimized whenever \( x \) lies between them, and that minimum equals the distance between the two points themselves: \( |[[a]] - [[b]]| = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This adds the two points instead of measuring the distance between them.
This takes the absolute value of the sum rather than the difference of the two points.
This is half the distance between the points, not the full distance.
This is off by one from the correct minimum.
This is off by one from the correct minimum.
This is off by two from the correct minimum.

stem: The equation \( |[[a]]x - ([[b]])| = [[c]] \) has two real solutions. What is their sum?
solution: The two solutions are \( x = \dfrac{[[b]] - [[c]]}{[[a]]} \) and \( x = \dfrac{[[b]] + [[c]]}{[[a]]} \); their sum is \( \dfrac{2([[b]])}{[[a]]} = [[answer]] \), since the \( [[c]] \) terms cancel.
distractor_rationales: This uses \( [[c]] \) (the distance) instead of \( [[b]] \) (the center) when forming the sum.
This forgets to double \( [[b]]/[[a]] \); both roots contribute a \( [[b]]/[[a]] \) term.
This forgets to divide by the coefficient \( [[a]] \) of \( x \).
This is off by one from the correct sum.
This is off by one from the correct sum.
This is off by two from the correct sum.
